title: ammonia::clean() used as URL sanitizer — javascript: scheme passes through
description: feed.rs uses ammonia::clean(url) to sanitize feed entry link URLs before injecting them into href attributes (lines 66-67, 73-74). ammonia::clean() is an HTML sanitizer — it strips tags and encodes HTML entities, but it does NOT validate or strip URL schemes. A feed entry with href='javascript:alert(1)' survives unchanged and is stored in the posts table as a clickable link. When followers open the post in a client, clicking the link executes JavaScript. Fix: validate that the URL scheme is https:// or http:// before injecting into HTML. Use url::Url::parse and check the scheme, reject or omit links that don't pass.
close_reason: All pass 2 fixes applied: circuit breaker reset, persona-scoped 410, SSRF private IP block, tokio::fs, URL scheme validation, Content-Type, page clamp, watch dedup
